mentions.note: Data infrastructure company; 22% of Fundrise Fund portfolio; only exciting holding in Robinhood Ventures Fund (7 stocks); Cramer calls it 'truly exciting'; Fundrise got in 2023 at lower valuation, Robinhood late 2025.

Caller Steve bought at $75 beginning of year, now at $45, down $30/share, asking whether to cut losses. Cramer says no; checked delinquencies (very low); calls it 'rocket ship'; down 40% doesn't make sense; backlog strong, good operator.
Creative software incumbent vulnerable to AI-native competitors; natural language and multimodal AI undercut Adobe's traditional complexity and lock-in.
Semiconductor manufacturing equipment supplier benefiting from massive AI compute buildout; Cramer calls out strong performance.

20%+ of Fundrise Innovation Fund holdings; called 'king of enterprise AI' by Cramer; frontier model driving enterprise transformation; major beneficiary of OpenAI's $120B round ecosystem (held by Fundrise since early rounds at lower valuations).
Enterprise software leader losing market share to private AI competitors; once revered, now in 'charnel house of pain'; Cramer warns of destruction in cohort.
Oil stocks rallying on Middle East conflict expectations, but unclear if war is truly escalating; not a conviction call given policy contradictions.
Enterprise software/monitoring incumbent losing favor to AI disruption; Cramer notes destruction in traditional software cohort.

Caller Scott (Minnesota): EQPT stock falling apart, not expensive. Cramer endorses: 'stock just too cheap, it's crazy'; recommends buying on weakness but not all at once; something's clearly wrong but price too low.
Energy stocks soaring on 82nd Airborne deployment and oil-price concerns, but Cramer unclear on true war trajectory; avoid trading.

Newly listed venture cap fund; launched March 6, dropped 16% on day 1, now back above IPO. NAV $25.31 vs. stock price ~$26 — reasonable but not compelling. Only 7 holdings, mostly weak except Databricks; Cramer prefers Fundrise.
Financials rallying on Trump's 'war won' comments and potential peace resolution, but Cramer uncertain given continued missile strikes.
Defense contractor down 15% in 3 weeks despite Trump's huge order awards; if war is ending (per Trump's rhetoric), Lockheed becomes less attractive; Cramer recommends avoiding.
Commodity stocks soaring due to polyethylene supply loss from Middle East disruption; not a conviction call given war uncertainty.
Enterprise software incumbent under siege from AI upstarts like OpenAI, Anthropic, Databricks; despite profitability, facing existential competitive threat.
Enterprise software incumbent vulnerable to AI disruption; Cramer notes these stocks were 'exalted winners' but now face predatory competition from OpenAI, Anthropic.
King of AI hardware thriving as private AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, Databricks) scale compute demands; ecosystem beneficiary of enterprise software disruption.

Historic $120B+ fundraising round at $840B valuation; fastest-growing company on Earth with 900M weekly active users, 50M+ subscribers; path to IPO late 2026/early 2027; profitable enterprise growth and consumer subscriptions; force-multiplier AI narrative proven with customer wins (BBVA, Lowe's, Walmart).
Caller Bill: bought PDYN at $9, now $6.50. Cramer: market thinks it will lose money next year; AI/ML should work but company losing money; not recommending loss-making stocks in 2026.
Caller Craig: asking about RACE. Was hot stock but lost mojo. Cramer: 'I don't like the stock'; sells at too high a price; doesn't see path to recovery.
Caller Steve (California): asking about SERV. Cramer says 'losing a fortune' and 'too complex at time'; recommends avoiding.

Caller Dustin: company posting first $1B+ quarter, record revenue, strong growth, 9 straight quarters GAAP profitable; CEO bought shares twice in March. Cramer: 'I'm a believer in SoFi'; bulls right despite controversy; knows CEO Anthony Noto since 1998, trusts his character.
5.5% of Fundrise Innovation Fund; aerospace/defense beneficiary; held by Fundrise at early valuations; part of diversified venture portfolio.
Enterprise software developer-tools company facing existential threat from AI agents and OpenAI's coding capabilities; once revered, now in decline.

Soared 1,000%+ in 4 sessions due to extreme lockup mechanics (90% of shares locked 6 months), not fundamentals. Trading at 16.5x NAV ($19 per-share NAV vs. $315 price) — pure lunacy. Expect sharp pullback when lockups expire in September.
Enterprise software leader in decline as AI upstarts capture value; expensive cloud/SaaS model losing appeal to cheaper AI-native solutions.
Consumer stocks surging alongside financials on peace narrative; highlighted as AI force-multiplier example by OpenAI CFO (merchandising, coding, consumer search).
Enterprise software/security company vulnerable to AI disruption; part of cohort losing market share to AI-native competitors.
